name: Astron Agent
tagline: Enterprise-grade platform for building production-ready agentic workflows
seoTitle: Astron Agent: Enterprise Agentic Workflow Platform
metaDescription: Astron Agent is an open-source, Apache-2.0 platform from iFLYTEK for building production-ready AI agents with workflow orchestration, RPA, and MCP tools.
overview: Astron Agent is an enterprise-grade, commercial-friendly platform for building agentic workflows. It brings together AI workflow orchestration, model management, AI and MCP tool integration, RPA automation, and team collaboration in one place, so organisations can build scalable, production-ready agent applications.

The platform is built on the same core technology as the iFLYTEK Astron Agent Platform and supports high-availability deployment. Its native RPA integration connects internal and external enterprise systems, letting agents move from a decision to a real action and close the loop. It is released under the Apache 2.0 License with no commercial restrictions.
features: Visual AI workflow orchestration for building and running agent applications
Model management with flexible large-model access, from API-based models to on-premises MaaS clusters
Native intelligent RPA integration for cross-system process automation
Ready-to-use tools from the iFLYTEK Open Platform, plus AI and MCP tool integration
Enterprise-grade high availability with one-click deployment
Team collaboration features for shared agent development

gettingStarted.intro: Astron Agent offers two deployment methods. The fastest way to get started is Docker Compose; a Helm chart for Kubernetes is in development. You can also try it without installing anything on Astron Cloud.

steps.heading: Clone the repository and open the Docker directory
steps.body: Clone the project from GitHub and move into the Docker deployment directory.

steps.heading: Set up the environment configuration
steps.body: Copy the example environment file and edit it with your own settings. See the project's deployment guide for the full list of variables.

steps.heading: Start all services
steps.body: Launch every service, including the Casdoor authentication service, with Docker Compose. The frontend is then served at http://localhost/ and the Casdoor admin interface at http://localhost:8000.

steps.heading: Or try it on Astron Cloud
steps.body: If you would rather not deploy anything, Astron Cloud gives you a ready-to-use environment for creating and managing agents at https://agent.xfyun.cn.

useCases: Building production-ready, scalable agent applications inside an enterprise
Automating cross-system business processes by connecting agents to internal and external systems through RPA
Managing and serving large models through API access or an on-premises MaaS cluster
Letting teams collaborate on shared agentic workflows with reusable AI and MCP tools
